name: Bevy Collegiate Bus Svc LLC
recent_news: No public web footprint found; no website, no news, no acquisition signals. Small Milwaukee LLC at residential-style address (910 N Renee St); D&B-style listing only.
fit_notes: Tiny (~37 employees, ~$1.5M rev) MKE-area operator with no digital footprint; likely subscale owner-operator. Low priority unless found to serve MPS choice/voucher routes.

name: Fascino Reno Which Will Do Bus
recent_news: No web footprint. Name appears to be a malformed D&B-style filing ('Which Will Do Bus' is filler text from registration). Residential address on Meadowview Ln, Reno. Washoe County School District runs its own ~358-bus in-house fleet, limiting private contractor opportunities.
fit_notes: Likely a micro owner-operator or activity/charter trip provider; WCSD self-operates so contract pipeline is thin. Low priority for Summit tuck-in.

name: MK Trans Inc Which Will Do Bus
recent_news: No web footprint, website, or news. Name 'Which Will Do Bus' is malformed registration filler. Residential address on Niagra Ct, Reno 89506 (Stead/north Reno). WCSD runs its own fleet.
fit_notes: Likely a micro owner-operator; no evidence of district contracts. WCSD self-performs transportation. Low priority for Summit.

name: Four Legged Kids Sch Bus LLC
recent_news: No web/news footprint. Registered at residential address (5414 Bettie Lou Ct, North Las Vegas 89031). CCSD operates its own large in-house transportation department in Las Vegas/Clark County.
fit_notes: Likely sub-contractor/owner-operator (possibly daycare/Pre-K shuttle given the 'kids' branding). CCSD self-operates routes, limiting upside. Very low Summit fit.

name: Bath Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: Regional School Unit 1 (RSU 1) - West Bath and Georgetown routes (Bath/Woolwich/Arrowsic/Phippsburg primary routes now run by First Student)
recent_news: 2020: Bill Reed (President) considered installing more cameras after a driver was charged with assault for pushing a 12-year-old Woolwich student; company ran ~30 buses at the time (Portland Press Herald). RSU 1 has shifted primary K-12 route contract to First Student; Bath Bus retains West Bath/Georgetown service.
fit_notes: Long-standing family-owned mid-coast Maine operator (~30 buses, ~45 employees) now de-scoped by RSU 1 to West Bath/Georgetown after First Student won main contract — classic succession/exit signal. Plausible Summit tuck-in to consolidate a niche route and absorb residual fleet/drivers in a Summit-adjacent New England market.
